step_by_step_thinking:
Analyzing the origins of the mentioned muscles according to embryological data, we find that the levator palpebrae superioris arises from the same somitic series as the extraocular muscles, specifically the third somite corresponding to the development of Cranial Nerve III (Oculomotor nerve), which includes the levator palpebrae superioris among others. On the contrary, the orbicularis oculi derives exclusively from the second pharyngeal arch, confirming it as distinct from those developed from somitic sources.
\boxed{D} Based on the detailed embryonic derivation information presented, the orbicularis oculi being uniquely associated with the second pharyngeal arch sets it apart from muscles whose development stems from somitic contributions linked to Cranial Nerve III, thereby excluding D (Orbicularis oculi) from sharing a developmental source with the other listed muscles.
Thus,
raw_response:
Analyzing the origins of the mentioned muscles according to embryological data, we find that the levator palpebrae superioris arises from the same somitic series as the extraocular muscles, specifically the third somite corresponding to the development of Cranial Nerve III (Oculomotor nerve), which includes the levator palpebrae superioris among others. On the contrary, the orbicularis oculi derives exclusively from the second pharyngeal arch, confirming it as distinct from those developed from somitic sources.
\boxed{D} Based on the detailed embryonic derivation information presented, the orbicularis oculi being uniquely associated with the second pharyngeal arch sets it apart from muscles whose development stems from somitic contributions linked to Cranial Nerve III, thereby excluding D (Orbicularis oculi) from sharing a developmental source with the other listed muscles.
Thus, \boxed{D}.
